003 * contributor license agreements.  See the NOTICE file distributed with
004 * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.
005 * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0
006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with
007 * the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at
008 *
009 *      http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
010 *
011 * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
012 * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
013 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
014 * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.commons.svl.functions;
018
019import org.apache.juneau.commons.svl.*;
020
021/**
022 * Boolean-logic and comparison functions for the {@code #{...}} script catalog.
023 *
024 * <p>
025 * Boolean coercion is explicit (no implicit truthiness). Comparison functions ({@code lt},
026 * {@code gt}, etc.) compare numerically; {@code eq} / {@code neq} compare strings.
027 */
028@SuppressWarnings({
029   "java:S1192" // Duplicate string literals are SVL operator names used in annotation/config keys; intentional
030})
031public final class BooleanFunctions {
032
033   private BooleanFunctions() {}
034
035   /** All function classes in this category. */
036   @SuppressWarnings({
037      "unchecked", // Cast is safe: type verified by caller context.
038      "java:S2386" // ALL is an immutable compile-time registry; exposed as an array for the cross-package/varargs functions(...) API, so visibility cannot be reduced.
039   })
040   public static final Class<? extends VarFunction>[] ALL = new Class[] {
041      And.class, Or.class, Not.class, Xor.class,
042      Eq.class, Neq.class, Lt.class, Lte.class, Gt.class, Gte.class
043   };
044
045   /** {@code #{and(a, b, ...)}} — returns {@code "true"} only if all args are true. Variadic. */
046   public static class And extends TypedFunction {
047      @Override public String name() { return "and"; }
048      public String invoke(String[] args) {
049         for (var a : args)
050            if (!parseBool(a)) return "false";
051         return "true";
052      }
053   }
054
055   /** {@code #{or(a, b, ...)}} — returns {@code "true"} if any arg is true. Variadic. */
056   public static class Or extends TypedFunction {
057      @Override public String name() { return "or"; }
058      public String invoke(String[] args) {
059         for (var a : args)
060            if (parseBool(a)) return "true";
061         return "false";
062      }
063   }
064
065   /** {@code #{not(a)}} — returns the boolean negation of {@code a}. */
066   public static class Not extends TypedFunction {
067      @Override public String name() { return "not"; }
068      public String invoke(boolean a) { return String.valueOf(!a); }
069   }
070
071   /** {@code #{xor(a, b)}} — returns {@code a XOR b}. */
072   public static class Xor extends TypedFunction {
073      @Override public String name() { return "xor"; }
074      public String invoke(boolean a, boolean b) { return String.valueOf(a ^ b); }
075   }
076
077   /** {@code #{eq(a, b)}} — string-equality. Returns {@code "true"} or {@code "false"}. */
078   public static class Eq extends TypedFunction {
079      @Override public String name() { return "eq"; }
080      public String invoke(String a, String b) { return String.valueOf(a == null ? b == null : a.equals(b)); }
081   }
082
083   /** {@code #{neq(a, b)}} — string-inequality. */
084   public static class Neq extends TypedFunction {
085      @Override public String name() { return "neq"; }
086      public String invoke(String a, String b) { return String.valueOf(!(a == null ? b == null : a.equals(b))); }
087   }
088
089   /** {@code #{lt(a, b)}} — numeric less-than. */
090   public static class Lt extends TypedFunction {
091      @Override public String name() { return "lt"; }
092      public String invoke(double a, double b) { return String.valueOf(a < b); }
093   }
094
095   /** {@code #{lte(a, b)}} — numeric less-than-or-equal. */
096   public static class Lte extends TypedFunction {
097      @Override public String name() { return "lte"; }
098      public String invoke(double a, double b) { return String.valueOf(a <= b); }
099   }
100
101   /** {@code #{gt(a, b)}} — numeric greater-than. */
102   public static class Gt extends TypedFunction {
103      @Override public String name() { return "gt"; }
104      public String invoke(double a, double b) { return String.valueOf(a > b); }
105   }
106
107   /** {@code #{gte(a, b)}} — numeric greater-than-or-equal. */
108   public static class Gte extends TypedFunction {
109      @Override public String name() { return "gte"; }
110      public String invoke(double a, double b) { return String.valueOf(a >= b); }
111   }
112
113   /** Same truthiness table as {@link ArgCoercer}, exposed for variadic use in {@link And}/{@link Or}. */
114   private static boolean parseBool(String s) {
115      var t = s == null ? "" : s.trim();
116      if (t.equalsIgnoreCase("true") || t.equals("1") || t.equalsIgnoreCase("yes") || t.equalsIgnoreCase("on"))
117         return true;
118      if (t.isEmpty() || t.equalsIgnoreCase("false") || t.equals("0") || t.equalsIgnoreCase("no") || t.equalsIgnoreCase("off"))
119         return false;
120      throw new IllegalArgumentException("cannot coerce '" + s + "' to boolean (accepted: true/1/yes/on, false/0/no/off, empty)");
121   }
122}
